assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states Classification: 'code change request' and Action: 'Sub-task created: subtask-30001.md'. The file subtask-30001.md exists in outputs. The report's Review Feedback Summary confirms: '30001 | reviewer-a | ... | code change request | Sub-task created (subtask-30001.md)'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states Classification: 'suggestion'. The Reasoning section explicitly states: 'The reviewer uses suggestive language: "should also add" and "would help." While the comment proposes a concrete change (adding a partial index), the phrasing frames it as an enhancement that would improve performance rather than a required correction.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states Classification: 'nit' and Action: 'No sub-task created. Nit-level feedback does not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states Classification: 'question' and Action: 'No sub-task created. Questions do not trigger sub-task creation -- they require the PR author’s response to determine whether a code change is needed.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 29) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 30).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 32) with Comment ID: 30001, Author: reviewer-a, File reference, and the full original review comment text quoted verbatim: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains exactly 6 files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, and report.md. All are classification outputs, sub-task descriptions, or the verification report. No .rs, .toml, .sql, or other code files exist in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have corresponding classification files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md. The report's Review Feedback Summary table lists all 4 comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action. The overall result is 'FAIL' due to scope containment issues, and the report only creates sub-tasks and documents findings. No merge command or merge recommendation appears anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files were modified or deleted. |' The detailed section confirms: 'New: `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` (62 lines, 5 test functions) -- Modified: none -- Deleted: none'.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section with three checks: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check finding no documented convention for index creation, (2) Codebase pattern check finding no countable pattern of index creation in migrations, (3) Performance-related scrutiny finding no performance conventions documented. The report's '#### Convention Upgrade -- PASS' section mirrors this analysis. Both conclude the suggestion does not match any documented or demonstrated project convention.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md Action states: 'No sub-task created. The suggestion is valid feedback but does not meet the threshold for a tracked code change request.' No subtask-30002.md exists in outputs. The Convention Upgrade Eligibility section concludes: 'Since no documented convention in CONVENTIONS.md matches and no demonstrated codebase pattern supports the upgrade, the suggestion remains classified as a suggestion.' The report confirms: '30002 | reviewer-a | ... | suggestion | No sub-task (no convention backs upgrade)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table states: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on the PR.' The detailed section '#### Eval Quality -- N/A' states: 'No eval result reviews detected on the PR. No reviews match the three-criteria detection (author = `github-actions[bot]`, body contains `## Eval Results`, body contains `sdlc-workflow/run-evals` footer).' The Test Quality row combines the sub-results without Eval Quality affecting it: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS... Eval Quality: N/A'.
